title: Russia is jamming GPS from space
summary: Russia is reportedly jamming GPS signals from space, posing a significant risk to American GPS systems. This development highlights concerns over potential high-impact GPS interference.

title: Quantum Space to go public in SPAC deal
summary: Quantum Space, a company led by a former NASA administrator, will go public through a merger with a SPAC. The company focuses on developing highly maneuverable spacecraft for national security missions.

title: WRC-27: the next arena for U.S.-China space competition
summary: China has filed for 200,000 additional satellites, signaling its aggressive push to dominate the space economy. This move sets the stage for heightened competition with the U.S. ahead of WRC-27.

title: Axiom and Prada advance design of spacesuit
summary: Axiom Space and Prada have collaborated to advance the design of a lunar spacesuit for NASA. This partnership combines Axiom's expertise in space technology with Prada's luxury design capabilities.

title: Speed as a Strategic Advantage in Border Monitoring
summary: Border monitoring in Europe is becoming more complex due to rapidly changing activities. Space-based solutions are being explored to provide strategic advantages in tracking these dynamic environments.

title: UK startup NewOrbit raises $18.5 million in Series A round
summary: UK startup NewOrbit Space raised $18.5 million in a Series A funding round to develop satellites for very low Earth orbit (VLEO). The investment will support the company's growth and satellite development efforts.

title: OQ Technology plans direct-to-smartphone demo with cellular spectrum
summary: OQ Technology is preparing to demonstrate direct-to-smartphone satellite connectivity in Germany next year using a local telco's cellular spectrum. This move aims to compete with U.S.-led satellite services in the emerging market.

title: NASA to select new headquarters building by end of year
summary: NASA is planning to select a new headquarters building by the end of the year, though it will remain in the Washington area. The decision aims to modernize the agency's workspace while maintaining its operational base.

title: Rounding up the space unicorns
summary: Billion-dollar space startups, once rare, are now becoming more common in emerging orbital markets. The article highlights the growth of these 'space unicorns' in the space industry.

title: NASA interested in Hubble reboost if costs can be reduced
summary: NASA is considering a reboost mission for the Hubble Space Telescope if the operating costs can be reduced. The agency is already preparing a similar attempt for another spacecraft in a decaying orbit.

title: Don't miss Jupiter and Venus meet in a dazzling conjunction on June 9: Here's where and when to look
summary: Jupiter and Venus will appear close together in the western sky on June 9 in a celestial conjunction. Details on how to observe the event are provided.

title: Astronaut spots Mount Vesuvius from space | Space photo of the day for June 8, 2026
summary: An astronaut captured a stunning space photo of Mount Vesuvius from orbit. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 8, 2026.

title: 'It's very aesthetically pleasing.' Prada and Axiom just revealed the stylish cooling suit Artemis astronauts will wear under their spacesuits on the moon
summary: Prada and Axiom Space unveiled a stylish cooling suit for Artemis astronauts to wear under their spacesuits on the moon. The liquid cooling ventilation garment aims to enhance comfort and functionality during lunar missions.

title: Rare daytime meteor shower peak this week — here's how to see (and even hear) them
summary: The Arietids meteor shower peaks this week, offering a rare chance to see shooting stars in the dawn sky instead of after dark. Observers may also hear the meteors as they pass by.

title: Is this the next Artemis crew? A look at the astronauts on NASA's shortlist
summary: NASA is set to reveal the astronauts for the Artemis 3 docking mission in Earth orbit next year on June 9. The announcement will highlight the contenders for the next Artemis crew.

title: 10 best Spanish beaches to see the total solar eclipse 2026
summary: On August 12, 2026, some of the world's best beaches in Spain will experience a total solar eclipse. This rare astronomical event is expected to attract significant attention from both locals and tourists.

title: 'Rick and Morty' creators on their inspirations as season 9 tackles the multiverse, evolution, and a kung-fu fight in a Trader Joe's parking lot (interview)
summary: The creators of 'Rick and Morty' discuss their inspirations for season 9, which explores themes of the multiverse, evolution, and a unique kung-fu fight in a Trader Joe's parking lot. They describe the season as a celebration, reflecting their creative vision.

title: Could meteor storms harm NASA's future moon missions?
summary: NASA may delay future moon missions if a major meteor shower outburst or storm is forecasted during crew activities. Crews would remain inside until the event passes.

title: Supersonic!
summary: NASA's experimental X-59 aircraft achieved its first supersonic flight on June 5, 2026, reaching Mach 1.1 with test pilot Jim 'Clue' Less. The 81-minute flight at Edwards Air Force Base marks a milestone ahead of planned quiet supersonic demonstrations later in the year.

title: Digging Back in Time in the UAE
summary: Jabal al Fāyah in the UAE was once submerged under a shallow sea, serving as a geological reminder of the region's ancient aquatic past. The site also highlights early human adaptation to changing environments.

title: NASA’s X-59 Aircraft Flies Supersonic for First Time
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft achieved supersonic flight for the first time, reaching Mach 1.1 during an 81-minute test flight. The milestone paves the way for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later in 2024.

title: NASA Announces Winners of 2026 University Innovation Competition
summary: NASA announced MIT as the first-place winner of the 2026 RASC-AL competition for their lunar power system project. A second MIT team and Virginia Tech also placed in the top three.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Research Continues on Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II Moon Mission research continues on Earth to support safe human exploration of deep space and provide a blueprint for future lunar surface science missions. The findings will help NASA build a Moon Base and establish an enduring human presence there.

title: NASA Concludes Antenna Mishap Investigation, Releases Report
summary: NASA has concluded the investigation into the damage to its Deep Space Station 14 (DSS-14) antenna at Goldstone, classifying it as a Type A mishap. The agency has released a report on the incident.

title: First Steps: America’s Grueling Second Spacewalk
summary: NASA conducted its second spacewalk one year after the first, with Gene Cernan performing the EVA during the Gemini IX-A mission on June 5, 1966. The spacewalk was originally planned for Gemini VIII but was reassigned after that mission ended early.

title: Fighting Fire With Fire
summary: Prescribed burns are used in Australia's Northern Territory to reduce the severity of wildfires during the fire season. This practice helps manage fire-prone ecosystems.

title: NASA Hosts 2026 Review on Advanced Composite Manufacturing
summary: NASA's Hi-Rate Composite Aircraft Manufacturing (HiCAM) project held a 2026 spring review at Langley Research Center, bringing together 150 consortium members to assess progress. The 22-member public-private partnership discussed advancements in advanced composite manufacturing.

title: NASA-Funded Study Shows Wildfire Smoke’s Hidden Ozone Toll
summary: A NASA-funded study reveals that wildfire smoke has significantly increased ground-level ozone pollution across much of the contiguous U.S. over the past decade, posing health risks even in areas far from active fires.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during a recent hotfire test at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on future objectives after the anomaly.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement marks a key milestone in the spacecraft's development.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Force is assessing damage and potential future risks from an anomaly. Space Launch Delta 45 has cleared a significant milestone in the evaluation process.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparations for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36 (LC-36) with the goal of returning the facility to flight status by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different providers. The launches highlight the growing competition in the global space industry.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic phenomena.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The anomaly marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions by the European Space Agency.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young contributors, test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and education.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or experiment, referred to as 'The great parachute bake-out'.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale VLEO-capable spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first NEO-1 satellite in 2028.

title: Juno Propulsion Raises $1.4M Pre-Seed for RDE Flight
summary: Juno Propulsion secured $1.4M in pre-seed funding to develop its first flight-ready rotating detonation engine (RDE), named Project Iris, with a demo mission planned for 2027.

title: Mass Is the Easy Part, Coordination Is The Hard Part
summary: Ukraine's use of cheap drones has forced the U.S. to adopt mass-produced, autonomous, and disposable systems to counter them, highlighting a shift in defense economics. The U.S. is now rapidly developing these technologies to match the scale of adversarial drone production.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate the company's capability to rendezvous with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised an additional $200 million, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This marks the company's third $200 million funding round in approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The US commercial space station industry is expanding into Europe as the ISS nears retirement. Europe's access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) will continue through these new commercial ventures.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights its differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. The discussion emphasizes Planet's approach to handling outcomes.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx capsule on May 19 to evaluate parachute performance and landing dynamics. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to simulate a controlled Earth landing.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times the size of its predecessor Condor-XL and targets the emerging orbital data center market.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation, allowing it to launch half of its satellites by the end of July with conditions on spectrum priority.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in funding, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, beginning with the PExT demonstration mission. The PExT mission, known as Polylingual Experimental Terminal, aims to enhance space communications.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services for maritime applications. Eutelsat is a major player in the satellite industry, and this deal expands its reach in the maritime sector.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for maritime domain awareness using AI-enabled data analytics. The contract builds on a previous agreement to support U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites, 'Tom & Jerry', for a maneuvering and inspection mission in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO). The mission aims to demonstrate space rendezvous capabilities.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics and NTT have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on developing a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership aims to enhance space communications capabilities.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform called 'Ultra' designed for orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for delivery in 2028.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite operations.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company is an aerospace and defense firm that recently went public.
